evals.prompt: I'm building a FastAPI app for a blog. Create a full Pydantic v2 schema set for a Post resource: Base, Create, Update, Response, and InDB models. The post has title, content, published status, author_id, and the DB adds id, created_at, updated_at. The Update model should support partial updates.
evals.expected_output: 5 model classes using inheritance. PostBase with shared fields. PostCreate inheriting PostBase. PostUpdate with all Optional fields (= None defaults). PostInDB and PostResponse with id/timestamps and ConfigDict(from_attributes=True). No orm_mode, no .dict(), no class Config block.

assertions.text: PostUpdate has all fields as Optional[T] = None (not just Optional[T] without default)
PostInDB and/or PostResponse use ConfigDict(from_attributes=True), NOT class Config: orm_mode = True
Uses model_config = ConfigDict(...) class variable, NOT a class Config: nested class
No deprecated v1 methods (.dict(), .parse_obj(), @validator) in the generated code
PostBase defines the shared fields (title, content, published) used by Create and Update

evals.prompt: I need validators on my UserCreate Pydantic model: email should be lowercase and stripped, password must be at least 8 chars and contain a digit, and confirm_password must match password. Show me the correct Pydantic v2 syntax for both field-level and cross-field validators.
evals.expected_output: Uses @field_validator with @classmethod for email and password. Uses @model_validator(mode='after') for password confirmation. No @validator (v1 syntax). Cross-field validator accesses self.password and self.confirm_password on the model instance.

assertions.text: model_validator accesses self.password and self.confirm_password (not cls and values dict)
Password validator raises ValueError if password has no digit

evals.prompt: I have a SQLAlchemy User ORM model and want to create Pydantic response schemas. I need: UserResponse (public fields only, no password_hash), a generic Page[T] model for paginated results, and a computed full_name field derived from first_name + last_name. Show me complete Pydantic v2 code.
evals.expected_output: UserResponse with ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) and no password_hash. Generic Page[T] using TypeVar and Generic[T]. @computed_field with @property for full_name. All v2 syntax.

assertions.text: UserResponse uses ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) to read from SQLAlchemy ORM objects
Page[T] uses Generic[T] and TypeVar — correct Python generic pattern
full_name uses @computed_field with @property decorator (NOT a plain @property or validator)
UserResponse does NOT include password_hash field
No class Config: orm_mode = True anywhere in the code
